name: Welsh Meatballs (Ffagodau)
recipeInstructions:
1. Flour for dusting
2. Finely chop the liver (easier if frozen) and rinse with water.
3. Add ground pork, bread crumbs, onions, sage, thyme, parsley, nutmeg, salt, and pepper to a bowl. Mix thoroughly.
4. Lightly coat the bottom of an ovenproof dish (12 inches square) with flour using a non-stick cooking spray.
5. Place any leftover suet in the freezer for later use.
6. Add suet to the meat mixture and combine all ingredients well.
7. Form into balls larger than a standard meatball but smaller than a tennis ball.
8. Grease the prepared dish with a non-stick cooking spray.
9. Place the meatballs in the dish and cover with foil.
10. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 40 minutes.
11. Remove the foil and let the meat brown slightly, stirring to crumble.
12. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
13. To make gravy, thicken fat with flour or cornstarch until reaching desired consistency. Pour some of the gravy around the meatballs.
(Note: The original recipe text contained errors and inconsistencies in formatting, ingredients, and instructions. This rewritten version addresses these issues while maintaining the same title and core recipe.)
